data.title: Wall Street trades lower as OpenAI misses targets and FOMC rate decision lurks
data.description:
Wall Street’s major averages traded lower on Tuesday, with the markets under some pressure as a report on OpenAI missing its own targets has spooked investors.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.5%, while the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.8%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was near flat%. 

From a sector-by-sector stance, five of the 11 S&P segments are in the green, with energy at the top. Going the other way, industrials has been the weakest performer. 

U.S. Treasury yields moved higher. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose to 3.83%, while the 10-year yield (US10Y) climbed to 4.36% and the 30-year yield (US30Y) advanced to 4.95%, reflecting upward pressure as investors assessed the macro backdrop. 

OpenAI (OPENAI) recently missed its own targets for new users and revenue, stumbles that have raised concern among some company leaders about whether it will be able to support its massive spending on data centers. 

On the earnings front, General Motors (GM) posted solid quarterly results and beat Wall Street’s profit expectations by nearly 30%. Coca-Cola (KO) topped estimates with its first-quarter earnings report. 

Geopolitical tensions remained in focus after Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi arrived in Russia, where he is expected to meet President Vladimir Putin, even as negotiations with the U.S. reached a stalemate over the weekend.

data.title: What analysts are saying about Coca-Cola after earnings
data.description:
Coca-Cola (KO) gained just above 5% in early trading on Tuesday as investors and analysts sized up the Q1 earnings update and management commentary on the year ahead.

UBS analyst Sanjeet Aujila thinks the organic sales beat led by volumes should be taken well, which was a key concern for investors going into the Q1 update. "We think investors will debate the extent to which the better volumes are coming at the expense of weak price/mix," he noted.  

TD Cowen analyst Robert Moskow characterized the Q1 results as further proof the company can achieve its growth algorithm with earnings flexibility.

RBC Capital Markets analyst Nik Mondi said Coca-Cola (KO) is in a better position than most peers because it has proven savvy at navigating exogenous shocks better. He noted that the only things to watch are the movement in the dollar, which tends to have an impact on KO's share price, and potential elevated inflation that could weigh on profitability. "We believe the company’s latest restructuring and organizational design changes will facilitate better allocation of resources, which should ultimately lead to better share gains and white space expansion," he advised.

Deutsche Bank analyst Claire Rodgers expects a positive investor response to the Coca-Cola (KO) report, noting the top-line strength that was fairly broad-based by region.

Seeking Alpha analyst Kenio Fontes said he has always viewed Coca-Cola (KO) as a quality compounder, but at the same time, felt that for some time now, the stock had been trading at a high valuation relative to the growth it delivers. "So seeing KO report 10% organic top-line growth really impresses me," he updated. "This shows that Coca-Cola is indeed managing to implement effective strategies to navigate a more complex market environment while continuing to grow, even as a behemoth. An example is North America, which saw 10% organic revenue growth, largely driven by concentrate sales, with very little coming from price increases or product mix, demonstrating the strength of the strategy focused on smaller products and specific products (such as zero-sugar Coke)," he added. Fontes mentioned that the organic sales strength came alongside a 12% non-GAAP operating income growth, which shows that the company continues to reinforce the operating leverage case.

data.title: Sysco beats sales expectations, but profits undermined by increased operating costs
data.description:
Sysco (SYY) saw its profits deteriorate in the company’s fiscal third quarter despite posting higher sales as higher operating expenses drove unadjusted net earnings down 15%.

On an adjusted basis, however, earnings were down a more modest 3.6% to $0.94 per share, meeting Wall Street’s expectations.

The lackluster profitability of the company was partially offset by a 4.6% increase in revenue, a 3.3% improvement in U.S. volume, and a 19% gain in free cash flow. However, Q3 sales of $20.5B missed expectations by $60M, contributing to a drop in shares at Tuesday’s open.

Product cost inflation was 2.8% at the total enterprise level, as measured by the estimated change in Sysco’s (SYY) product costs, primarily in the dairy, meat, and seafood categories. Additionally, a 6.5% increase in Q3 gross profit was primarily driven by positive volumes, strategic sourcing efficiencies, and effective management of product cost inflation.

By segment, Sysco’s (SYY) Foodservice business realized a 3.1% increase in sales, 2.3% gain in total case volume, and a 3.3% increase in U.S. Foodservice operations. This resulted in a 5.2% gain in gross profit and a 38 basis point gain in gross margin.

In its International Foodservice Operations segment, sales increased 12.4%, while gross profit was 14.6% higher. Gross margin in the segment increased 41 basis points to 21.5%.

These results led the company to expect full-year adjusted EPS to be at the high end of its $4.50 to $4.60 range, which includes a ~$100M headwind ($0.16 per share) from lapping lower incentive compensation in FY25. This compares to estimates of $4.59 per share.

data.title: Richmond Fed Manufacturing Index rises more than expected in April
data.description:
April Richmond Fed Manufacturing Index: 3 vs. 2 consensus and 0 in March, according to data the Federal Reserve of Richmond released on Tuesday.

Developing… Check back for updates.

data.title: BridgeBio gains after settlement in Pfizer Vyndamax patent dispute
data.description:
BridgeBio (BBIO) rose 6.6% after a court filing disclosed a settlement in a generic patent case over Pfizer's (PFE) Vyndamax drug. Shares of Pfizer ticked higher by 0.8%.

Pfizer (PFE) and generic drug maker Hikma agreed to stipulate and agreed that Pfizer’s action against Hikma is hereby dismissed without prejudice, according to a court filing  on Tuesday.

The settlement with Hikma comes after Pfizer (PFE) agreed to settle last week with two other drugmakers it had sued over a patent dispute over its Vyndamax drug. The settlements come as a patent trial was slated to start this week.

Barclays analyst Eliana Merle on Friday said she sees base case settlement outcome could be a generic Vyndamax entry in 2031. BridgeBio (BBIO) makes a drug similar to Vyndamax/tafamidis called Attruby for a deadly form of heart disease.

"We think BBIO shares could be up ~30-40% (PFE settles/wins scenario) vs down ~20-30% (PFE loses scenario); though we think regardless of outcome this could still be a clearing event and shares could recover from there," Merle, who has an overweight rating and $157 price target on BBIO, wrote in a note on Friday.

data.title: Wells Fargo declares $0.45 dividend
data.description:
• Wells Fargo (WFC) declares $0.45/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.22%
  • Payable June 1; for shareholders of record May 8; ex-div May 8.
  • See WFC Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Infleqtion wins $1M Navy deal to boost QuIRC AI platform; shares slip 5%
data.description:
• Infleqtion (INFQ) announced on Tuesday that it won a $1M contract from the U.S. Navy to advance its QuIRC machine learning platform for RF signal processing.
  • The tech runs on Infleqtion’s GPU-based CML system, helping capture patterns in massive datasets while cutting computing and storage needs.  
  • The company will now upgrade the QuIRC with self-learning features; it was the only firm selected to move from Phase I to Phase II.
  • However, following the news, Infleqtion (INFQ) stock fell ~5.04% to $12.63.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Itron slides after Q2 outlook falls short of expectations
data.description:
Itron (ITRI) shares were down 8% during early trading on Tuesday after reporting a second quarter 2026 outlook that fell short of market expectations.

For the quarter ended March 31, Itron reported first-quarter adjusted earnings per share of $1.49 versus the consensus estimate of $1.24. GAAP EPS was $1.18 compared to the $1.10 estimate.

Revenue for the quarter decreased 3% year over year to $587M, which was more than the $572M consensus. 

However, for the quarter in progress, Itron expects revenue ranging from $560M to $570M, which is less than the $607M consensus. It expects adjusted EPS ranging from $1.25 to $1.35, which is also less than the $1.46 estimate.

"Itron's first quarter results were ahead of our expectations on strong execution and certain projects running ahead of schedule, resulting in record gross profit," said CEO Tom Deitrich. "Our utility customers are prioritizing resiliency and affordability. This multi-year investment trend to add intelligence to the grid is structural and aligns well with Itron's leading positions in essential networks, analytics, and operational intelligence applications."

The first quarter earnings results came out just two weeks after the technology-based utilities and infrastructure solutions company was notified that an unauthorized third party gained access to certain of its systems. 

Itron took action to remove the unauthorized activity and has not seen any further unauthorized activity within its corporate systems. The company noted that it launched an investigation with the support of external advisors and had also informed law enforcement. Itron does not believe the incident has had or is likely to have a material impact on the company.

Industry peers such as Advanced Energy Industries (AEIS) and Xylem (XYL) were both down about 3% on Tuesday.

data.title: Visa earnings watch: Markets bet on global growth and digital payments
data.description:
Shares of Visa (V) are drawing increased attention as investors position ahead of the company’s upcoming earnings release, scheduled after the closing bell. The stock has faced a challenging start to the year, down roughly 10.8% year-to-date and currently trading near the $312 level.

Market participants are increasingly turning to prediction platform Kalshi for insight into sentiment surrounding the report. Traders are actively pricing in expectations for the themes most likely to surface during management’s commentary, offering a window into how the market is framing Visa’s near-term narrative. 

Among the primary areas of focus are developments in blockchain integration, which could signal the company’s evolving role in digital payments infrastructure. Additionally, commentary tied to the upcoming World Cup is expected to highlight cross-border spending trends and global transaction volumes, historically a tailwind for Visa.

Outlined below is what traders on Kalshi are forecasting Visa to emphasize in its upcoming earnings call: 

  • International — 98% chance. 
  • Tokenization — 95% chance.
  • World Cup — 76% chance. 
  • Blockchain — 75% chance.
  • Hyperscaler — 67% chance. 
  • Regulation / Regulatory — 60% chance.
  • Inflation — 48% chance. 
  • Loyalty — 30% chance.
  • Cybersecurity — 24% chance. 
  • M&A / Merger — 24% chance.
  • Rate Cap / 10% Cap — 23% chance. 
  • Southwest — 21% chance. 
  • Holiday — 20% chance. 
  • Mercuryo — 9% chance.
  • Trump — 9% chance.

data.title: United Community CFO Harralson to retire, search for successor underway
data.description:
• United Community Banks (NYSE: UCB) said on Tuesday that Chief Financial Officer Jefferson Harralson will retire after nearly a decade with the company.
  • Harralson will remain in his role through the end of the year to support a transition, while the bank has engaged Korn Ferry to conduct a nationwide search for his successor.
  • Source: Press Release
